observed_anomaly_cues: leads are visibly bent out of alignment
failure_mechanism: Improper tooling alignment during lead forming.
mechanism_explanation: Improper tooling alignment can cause the leads to be bent during the forming process. The misalignment, even by a small margin, results in uneven force distribution, causing the leads to deform. This is exacerbated by tool wear and excessive line speed, which increase stress on the leads.
defect_region: leads at the bottom of the transistor
severity_visual_description: Leads are noticeably bent but not broken or severed.
corrective_action: Realign and maintain tooling, and reduce line speed.
counterfactual_prediction: Leads appear straight and evenly spaced after corrective action.
